---
version: "v1.0.5"
category: "bug-fixes"
---

#### Stale generated files are removed when generated paths change or disappear

Files emitted by `generate` blocks (and the `remote_state.generate` attribute) are now tracked in
`.terragrunt-generate-manifest` inside the cache working directory. On every run, files recorded
by the previous manifest that are not requested by the current configuration are removed before
OpenTofu/Terraform parses the working directory.

This fixes a class of confusing failures where a `generate` block was removed from a Terragrunt configuration
(or its `path` changed) but the previously generated `.tf` file lingered in the cache and was
picked up by the next `tofu init`/`plan`/`apply`.

Files left in place by `disable = true` with `if_disabled = "skip"` are still preserved across
runs when the block keeps the same path, so opting out of generation without removing the block
keeps the existing file untouched.
